summary: Core's Aluula 3-strut big air and kiteloop machine with a 14-point fixed bridle. Extremely high aspect ratio, thin LE, and no-deformation airframe for lightning-fast kite speed and smooth consistent loops. The playful counterpart to the XR Pro.
best_for: Big air, smooth kiteloops, and competitive freeride

sources.summary: The Aluula fixed-bridle combo is special. Loops are butter-smooth and the boost is impressive. Feels like a refined, more playful version of the XR Pro.
Best Core kite for riders who want Aluula performance without going full XR Pro aggression.

reviews.video_title: Big Air Kite Review 13 - Core Pace Pro
reviews.excerpt: hello and welcome to another plus-sized kite review where in today's much requested episode I do my best to cover the new core Pace Pro as a core kite owner myself with XR 6 and SS in my old quiver and some experience uh using my father's Nexus uh fours I believe I do have a fair bit of experience w
reviews.verdict: in summary although high performance and a great debut for a new model kite that already has a world champion in Zar hugan rad riding it this kite really feels like something designed for those higher level Riders who really love that core feeling or perhaps um are coming from an older model kite looking to get into their first alula kites but it may likely be a bit of a handful to get used to for riders coming from other top tier mod performance alova kites or just looking to progress into Big
reviews.full_transcript: hello and welcome to another plus-sized kite review where in today's much requested episode I do my best to cover the new core Pace Pro as a core kite owner myself with XR 6 and SS in my old quiver and some experience uh using my father's Nexus uh fours I believe I do have a fair bit of experience with the brand and general feel of the previous kites albeit from a few years back so since this kite first started to appear on the competition circuit right from its prototype phase I have been super excited to give it a blast not to mention the sales pitch core and its team have been promoting about its performance since release the idea of potential XR level power in an easierto use model with friendlier Loops just sounded like my potential dream kite to be honest but of course dreams are free and kite reality I seem to find time and time again is that if you improve one area of performance you are usually going to have to sacrifice somewhere else but by how much is always a question especially with topend alula price tags one more item of note though before we get into the writing I should also mention this uh delicious kite was purchased from Mike McDonald shom bigair kite.com so go check that out it's a great site with a destination guide I particularly like to to browse and some fun merch uh and if you've ever been to Cape Town for a big air season their shop in Dolphin Beach Hotel is pretty epic as well and definitely worth a visit before your session at the iconic spot as I mentioned in several videos before I love the work that Mike does for the industry and to be honest core in general seem to consistently put out some of my favorite YouTube content between Mike himself and Steven aerik uh doing the bulk of the best howtos for the more advanced level writing um also formerly Anton chernishov known as Advocate who used to do some great big air jump progression stuff and I think he was also a teacher in Cape Town um so I'll put some links to their channels in the description below they even have a vlogger i quite enjoy Ben beol who's always popping up in cool locations to shoot fun looking sessions that uh seem to add more spots to my kite bucket list now uh with that all aside let's get on to the writing I've managed to log about 4 hours of ride time in total on the pace spread out over three sessions in varying wind strengths and with each session I have either been able to compare it right away to some of the other top kites I've been riding lately or by riding with a group of friends of similar level and size on kites where I know how well they can perform particularly in terms of power Comfort level tricks Etc so although I have a pretty good idea how this kite Compares with my own experience for the sake of accuracy for this review I'm actually going to cheat a little bit and use some feedback of a buddy of mine who actually owns the kite I tried this here is Dave AKA sliderman AKA dark slide Dave AKA The Shepherd who had his first session with me on his 9 M Pace right here in New Zealand but since then has also been r fting a quiver of them for the last couple months in perhaps the most ideal circumstances to review mid to strong powered winds uh having been in the northern lagoons and beaches of Northeast Brazil now for me here in New Zealand the three sessions I had on this pace were a bit of a mixed bag with winds starting with a slightly up and down 20 to 30 knot session at mirow where you would have seen a lot of my reviews come from then Weeks Later a fairly disappointing ing 20 to 25 knot session at a spot in Auckland called takapuna and then finally a glorious 2 hours in 25 to 35 knots of banging wind at a local Flatwater spot we call Miola which is a spot we all love here in ockland but rarely get big winds for because it requires a direction from the Northwest that rarely delivers in Auckland sadly with a new kite I will also just mention there is a cool new core bar the sensor 4 Pro Pro which you do sadly need to upgrade to if you are going to be using this pace um at least if you want full safety flag good options available while you ride as I have mentioned before in my previous videos I think the sensor 3 Pro is one of the best bars on the market in terms of functionality and ease of use with my only dislikes about being that I'm not a fan of the grip and that the parts need regular replacement I found um every 8 months or so with this sensor 4 or the grip has improved a little bit and there is a lot of cool uh detail in its overall design but it does seem a little bit more complicated which I am assuming is to make it compatible with both old and new core kites for example if you look here count the four center lines coming through the bar up to where the high Y split is it seems like Overkill but I guess it was the only way they could make it work work with both pulley and non- pulley Bridal kites in the whole core range it does seem to create um a little bit of added drag but not significant um I just found it a bit annoying to set up mainly so a little bit more mindfulness when laying out the lines and you'll be all good to go so here we get onto our first session on the water which was actually both Dave and I's first time trying the kite it was uh nice and fresh out the bag which is always a blast but unfortunately it wasn't really the best intro to this kite for us for a few reasons mainly though the lack of wind we discovered for this 9 M size um to really make it sing even though the average was around 22 to 25 knots and it was gusting 30 at times so Dave started out on his new pace of course to Christen it with a maiden send and I started out on the slingshot NXT which uh is a must watch review I've done recently um as I figured this would be a great first comparison kite for the pace based on the information we' been given so far about it and the way it was marketed uh but despite me being at least 15 kg heavier than Dave it seemed I was noticeably more powered than he was and then when we swapped kites the reverse was in effect the NXT provided better low end when the wind was in the low 20s and more lift and Hang Time when the wind was strong enough to do some decent jumps while I was on the pace my first impressions were of course that it lacked the low end I was expecting from an alula kite a gripe I also had with the orbit Ultra if you've watched that video but I was also not thrilled with how the kite felt through the bar in that it felt a bit heavier on the bar um and a bit more heavy to maneuver compared to the NXT and it didn't feel quite as direct as I would have hoped for on the plus side however the kite was very fast particularly through the loops far faster than the NXT but because of the bar being possibly the heaviest ulite I've flown I probably even heavier than the recent North orbit Ultra I reviewed like with the ultra it was challenging to get your timing right and get a good feel for the correct amount of pressure to use in that first session so I would often overdo it and it made getting the time in quite challenging to such a point it really sa my confidence to try any tricks uh with the kite on this session other than a few basic boosts and Loops I even crashed a simple standard loop with the kite um when it didn't give me too much of a catch on the heli Loop a little bit because I overs sent it but I also just didn't find it very forgiving on the helies so I ended up hurting myself a bit uh in a way that lingered for a few weeks and cost me at least one more potential 9M testing Day on this kite sadly thankfully With The Wind that day it was at least gusting up to 30 knots a time so the session wasn't a total failure for reviewing and I was able to jump over 10 m and get some decent Loops in like this one around 12 M but I didn't really enjoy the first session on the kite at least not to compared to some of the recent kites I've reviewed where I just wanted to stay on them for hours um and very similar strength wins to this uh I got about about 45 somewhat frustrating minutes on the pace um then decided to just go back to the NXT where I had a blast and I actually gave Dave my 10 meter Evo DLB to make the most of the wind we had because this was also his first time in New Zealand and his first session ever at mwise so we wanted to make it a good one that initial feel was somewhat reminiscent of my experiences with my XR 7s um if you go back and watch that first comparison video of mine I talk about that when I use them um even though I own the kites it would always take me a good half hour to get used to how they fly uh particularly coming from my other quiver of orbits mainly because of the bar pressure and its relationship to the speed and power of the kite with this core Pace although the pressure is certainly a lot lighter than those older XR 7s it still feels heavier than what else I've been riding and testing lately which is not a characteristic I particularly love with core or kites if I'm honest I assume must just be something that core Riders love or have something to do with the way they perform because it has always been something I associate with their kites so to some extent it makes sense that they couldn't make this model too much different at the risk of upsetting their loyal customer base coming from other type Brands uh it's just a very unique and challenging feature I don't really understand to be honest because we didn't love our initial experience with the pace we actually didn't bother to take it out with us for a few more sessions um because I also had other kites ready to test and I wasn't sure if I wanted to keep going with this review uh so it took me another month or so before I could get back out in the pace once I had finished the Thrive review which I really enjoyed making and in that time Dave headed off to Brazil for a few months of riding there with him gone I was lucky enough to borrow another 9M Pace from a local Rider here in Ackland for the next few sessions and as I mentioned I'll also cover Dave's experiences so far with his is quiver moving on to my second session on the pace unfortunately another one that wasn't ideal for it as the wind was only around 20 to 27 knots and although my friends were riding around on their 9 M orbits and I did go for a larger board to help a little the pace just wasn't enough for me to do very much except test how low I could safely send some Loops basically once again the weight of the kite through the bar made it require a little more effort than I was used to too um so the session was not so enjoyable either and only lasted a half hour but I must say it was quite impressive how little height you needed to Loop the kite cleanly finally here we go with session number three and we got a proper 25 to 35 knots so things were really looking exciting to get a legit feel for what this kite was all about and I must say I was finally really excited about getting it out on the water now after the the safest launch in all of Auckland you can see from this initial footage of me riding up wind to the spot that the wind is pumping and the kite is just dying to shoot forward into the window which is always a good indication of how well it's going to shoot up to 12 if you can get your timing down with a send although I must say it was a bit nerve-wracking going out because of how much this kite was moving around um and how challenging I found controlling it like with my SS if you are not used to it the added weight on the bar the speed of the kite and the slightly less direct feel uh can give you the impression the wind may be a bit stronger than it is at times too and just takes a bit of getting used to during the more lit parts of this session I certainly felt like I finally had a lot of power on this guy and the weight and energy coursing through my hands as I rode around on it finally made me feel like uh I could imagine it being designed by the descendants of large Northern Viking types likely burying their ax in the skulls of lesser Town folk with that power and this wind I had no trouble jumping in the 15 to 8 meter range uh with my highest Loops coming in around the 12 to 14 M range in height I also tested a variety of different types of Loops some smaller with a higher kite angle to see if it would give me confidence to try some rotations on the kite and although and although the loops themselves didn't have a ton of power on them for me the kite still felt somewhat erratic and hard to get the timing down my perceived response from the bar to the kite felt less the direct than some of the recent kites I've ridden perhaps due to the relationship between the bar pressure and the speed of the kite um and the kite's speed of course is incredibly quick um as I mentioned like the orbit Ultra the bar pressure to kite speed just feels tricky to get the hang of for me but at least with this pace the pull of the kite is a lot less I believe than the orbit Ultra so I would say you could get a bit more confident on it quicker once you got used to that pressure and response time I was finding that with the excellent speed of the kite if I could get the timing right I would aggressively shoot off the water for some decent height in my jumps but and I wasn't finding there was too much added gifted lift like I would get from my evos or perhaps the NXT for example so in this session although I was riding it pretty lit uh I wasn't really getting any more height relative to my friends on their orbits um I think in this session I'd have actually gotten more height if I had have been on my 8m EVO lab or NXT um and possibly more if I was on a another 9 M like the Thrive or the sling or the sorry fly Surfer era to be honest um but that could just be due to challenges I have with controlling this kite I was also having a bit of trouble figuring out the right technique for safe and easy catching heli Loops like the Harlem kites particularly the force because the kite Loops so tightly if you just go for a high and tight heli Loop above your head you seem to get almost no lift as you to send so you can fall out of the sky a bit even while you're going through the heli Loop so it's quite important to either send the kite a little wider for a larger swooping heli Loop or I was also finding I could get a little bit of lift with the high and tight Loops if I sheet it out and then sheet it back in quickly after the loop to force a little bit of lift as I re-engage the bar after the loop to put it frankly even on this session I still just struggled with this kite and didn't really have the best time because frankly it made me feel like I was suddenly much worse at kiting and my skills and confidence had regressed but I was determined to get footage and really try my best to understand the kite however despite 2 hours on the water I did come off just having not really enjoyed myself perhaps a little bit given how much my friends were all having Stoke with the session uh conditions at this spot we don't get to ride so often it was after this session that I had another good chat with Dave about he was finding the kites after 2 months of riding them and thankfully he is at a much better experience than I did with my sessions uh so that's why I'm going to get a lot of his feedback as well Dave has come from a quiver of orbits and a couple of ocean Rodeo flights I believe and upgraded to this pace quiver with 8 10 and 12 M Kites and here's the feedback he's given me although it also took him some time to get used to the bar pressure and the flight pattern he has found that he particularly loves both the 10 and 12 meter Paces so far likely because he has also found that he basically needs to go a kite size up from what everyone else is riding where he is which means if they are all going 9 m in 20 to 35 knots he's taking the 10 the adjustment has meant that he is seeing good uh improvements in his height about 2 to 3 m from what he would have expected to get on his orbits in the same conditions and same kite size he loves how fast the kite moves and gets around the loops even on the slower wing tip sections especially in the larger sizes regardless of the size he is riding compared to the orbit he is finding the kites do have quite a yank so he's finding it can be both fun but a bit intimidating as well he's not being super stoked on the lowend friend or hangtime mind you as someone who specializes in board off related tricks he's finding he really needs to rush that board back on at times when he's not staying in the air as long as he'd like in terms of progression he's made a lot since he's been there and riding these kites perhaps of course because of the added height but it's also likely because of the conditions he's in being quite perfect a lot of the time in my opinion I think you would have had improved just as much if not more uh on most of the other kites I've been trying recently but he is very happy with the results and he is happy with his purchase in the larger sizes in particular he's heading off to Cape Town next um so he will get a little bit more time to test the 8 and N which he's not loved so far but I believe he is considering swapping the 8 m to an XR Pro or something else with a bit more power to be able to get more height in that size he'll likely also add a six meter Pace to his quiver to improve his double Loop game so with his feedback and my experiences let's cover the main aspects of the kite and their ratings so first off boosting and as we both found not bad at all but not quite up there with the top tier boosters the kite does have some XR feel to it and can really power you off the water so it has a lot of potential for technically sound Riders but it doesn't make it easy unless you're quite in tune with it I would say it has more potential than your standard orbits uh or orbit Pros but perhaps not quite as much as that here with the kites like the era Thrive or Vex so perhaps similar to the orbit Ultra it isn't that great either in the hangtime department as we both mentioned so probably not the ideal kite for Pure airstyle or board off progression as Dave is point out unless you are already proficient there for looping well this is where the kite gets its top marks and really shines and after the Harlem Thrive or Force I would say it's the fastest looping kite I have tested but I did find it a bit more technical to use in terms um of your timing um for some of the issues I mentioned earlier and with the nature of its hel loops and timing safe catches I wouldn't put it as one of the better kites get into kite loops with more to progress once you're already there uh unless of course you're coming from Kites like the XR which of course is dramatically easier uh to loop on than the XR for confidence building well again not really great marks from me after only a few sessions you can see in the last sessions I didn't even feel confident jumping in both directions um but I do have to bump it up in the ratings because of DA's feedback and his um experiences after a lot more time on the kite so perhaps not an ideal kite to just demo only once but if you stick with it you'll hopefully be rewarded for that time spent adjusting to its style in terms of fun Factor well Dave is clearly having a blast on his 10 and 12 but I can't say I really enjoyed the few sessions I had on the nine perhaps I was just not dialed in enough to it yet so once again a bit of mixed reviews here so in summary although high performance and a great debut for a new model kite that already has a world champion in Zar hugan rad riding it this kite really feels like something designed for those higher level Riders who really love that core feeling or perhaps um are coming from an older model kite looking to get into their first alula kites but it may likely be a bit of a handful to get used to for riders coming from other top tier mod performance alova kites or just looking to progress into Big Air because of my limited time on this pace it's hard for me to say if it is very versatile for other disciplines I don't think it would work so well for freestall nor non-looping free ride where you probably want to go for the core XR from the range instead uh so for me it's best used as a more technical Loop progression machine um if you already have a moderate level of skill I have been thinking that if I had not tested some of the last few kites I've done that I would like it a lot more um and if I'm being self-critical this kite just needed a lot more time for me uh to give it a fair Shake I think but that is time with the lack of wind we've had lately that I don't really have to spare especially um when I didn't really love the sessions I did have um and I might be overly critical here but I have come to the conclusion that for me personally if I'm paying for alula prices I either want the highest jumping the largest wind range the most versatile or perhaps the easiest to use for the extra money if you look at say the Evo DB that kite sure isn't one of the easier to use but it's certainly one of the highest boosting and probably has the best wind range of any kite on the market so I get a lot more sessions out of the respective sizes um and even after a year I still feel like I'm getting a lot of added performance for the stupid amount of money I spent on my quiver I can say as well that based on the many dozens of DMS I've received since I posted some clips of me testing this kite that a lot of other Riders have been finding similar experiences to Dave and I some absolutely love this kite and I can kind of see why but others being a bit more like me who recognize its performance potential but just don't find it very easy or fun to get the hang of so if you have any experiences and you think I've been too critical um or even if I've been fair in this review I'd love to hear your feedback in the comments and as always if you are new to the Channel please like And subscribe and you can check out my Instagram to see what I'm currently testing uh when the wind permits it so until next time goodbye
